en.extract:
The Sri Lanka scimitar babbler or Ceylon scimitar babbler (Pomatorhinus melanurus) is an Old World babbler. It is endemic to the island of Sri Lanka and was formerly treated as a subspecies of Indian scimitar babbler. The nominate form is found in the western part of wet hill regions of Sri Lanka, while race holdsworthi is found in the dry lowlands and eastern hills.


In culture

Most scimitar babbler species are referred as parandel kurulla by the Sinhala-speaking community. The term 'parandel' refers to dried grass and probably refers to the color of the bird. The vernacular name of the bird parandel kurulla roughly translates to English as 'dried-grass-colored bird'. This bird appears in a 4.50 rupee Sri Lankan postal stamp.


References


External links
Internet Bird Collection

The '''Sri Lanka scimitar babbler''' or '''Ceylon scimitar babbler''' ('''''Pomatorhinus melanurus''''') is an [[Old World babbler]]. It is endemic to the island of [[Sri Lanka]] and was formerly treated as a subspecies of [[Indian scimitar babbler]].<ref>Collar, NJ (2006) A partial revision of the Asian babblers (Timaliidae). Forktail 22:85–112 [http://people.pwf.cam.ac.uk/cns26/NJC/Papers/Babbler_revision.pdf PDF]</ref> The nominate form is found in the western part of wet hill regions of Sri Lanka, while race ''holdsworthi'' is found in the dry lowlands and eastern hills.<ref>Kaluthota, CD (2009) Sri Lanka Scimitar Babbler - A recent addition to the endemic birds of Sri Lanka. Siyoth 2(1):35-37
[http://www.fogsl.net/Prod/wp-content/uploads/2009/03/siyoth-2-pg35-37.pdf PDF] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110723130600/http://www.fogsl.net/Prod/wp-content/uploads/2009/03/siyoth-2-pg35-37.pdf |date=2011-07-23 }}</ref>

==In culture==
[[File:Sri Lanka Scimitar-babbler (cropped).jpg|thumb|left|200px]]
Most scimitar babbler species are referred as ''parandel kurulla''<ref>{{cite journal|title=Vernacular Names of the Birds of the Indian Subcontinent|journal=Buceros|author=Anonymous|year=1998|volume=3|issue=1|url=http://www.bnhsenvis.nic.in/pdf/vol%203%20(1).pdf|pages=53–109|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100401015111/http://www.bnhsenvis.nic.in/pdf/vol%203%20%281%29.pdf|archive-date=2010-04-01}}</ref> by the [[Sinhala language|Sinhala]]-speaking community. The term 'parandel' refers to dried grass and probably refers to the color of the bird. The [[vernacular]] name of the bird ''parandel kurulla'' roughly translates to English as 'dried-grass-colored bird'. This bird appears in a 4.50 [[rupee]] [[Sri Lanka]]n postal stamp.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.birdtheme.org/country/srilanka.html|title = Birds on stamps: Sri Lanka}}</ref>
[[Image:PomatorhinusMelanurusLegge.jpg|thumb|right|Two birds (above) with an [[ashy-headed laughingthrush]]]]

zh.extract:
斯里兰卡钩嘴鹛（学名：Pomatorhinus melanurus），是斯里兰卡特有的画眉科钩嘴鹛属鳥類，栖息地於亚热带或热带的湿润低地林或山地林。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。


参考文献
